request_debug.prompt_preview:
You are the final consolidation step in a startup slopmortem pipeline. Earlier stages compared a fresh pitch against several historical startup failures and produced per-failure lessons. Your job: pick and rank the lessons that actually apply to THIS pitch, dedup paraphrases, and explain why each applies.

Rules:
- Only consolidate lessons from the input list. Do NOT invent new risks. If a theme spans multiple lessons, write one canonical summary covering them.
- Drop lessons that do not apply t

response.text: {"top_risks":[{"summary":"Build your monetization layer before AWS/GCP/Azure ship native ephemeral preview environments.","applies_because":"The pitch targets 'ephemeral preview environments across cloud providers' — exactly the feature surface AWS, GCP, and Azure are expanding into natively, mirroring how Firebase/Amplify commoditized Parse.","raised_by":["parse::devtools","rethinkdb::devtools"],"severity":"high"},{"summary":"Define a concrete paid-tier trigger for CLI users before free usage becomes the default expectation.","applies_because":"The pitch ships a CLI plus a hosted control plane — the same CLI-then-hosted split that let RethinkDB users default to free; without an explicit upgrade trigger (e.g., concurrency limits, SSO, team seats), conversion stalls.","raised_by":["rethinkdb::devtools","parse::devtools"],"severity":"high"},{"summary":"Validate willingness-to-pay with at least 10 paying teams before scaling developer community growth.","applies_because":"The pitch is developer-focused B2B SaaS with a free CLI entry point — the exact pattern where community adoption preceded monetization in both historical failures, leading to irreversible free-tier expectations.","raised_by":["rethinkdb::devtools"],"severity":"high"},{"summary":"Sign multi-tenant enterprise contracts early to reduce churn from small developer teams.","applies_because":"The pitch explicitly targets developers (small-team entry point) with a hosted control plane — Parse's revenue was over-concentrated in small teams that churned, and the pitch describes the same customer profile.","raised_by":["parse::devtools","rethinkdb::devtools"],"severity":"high"},{"summary":"Ship migration tooling and open-source optionality from day one to preserve trust and avoid lock-in backlash.","applies_because":"The pitch offers a hosted control plane managing cloud infrastructure — vendor lock-in risk is concrete; if the service winds down or is acquired, developers with no escape hatch will distrust the platform, as Parse's shutdown demonstrated.","raised_by":["parse::devtools"],"severity":"medium"},{"summary":"Anchor pricing on operational pain solved, not on feature parity with incumbent cloud tooling.","applies_because":"The pitch competes on 'managing ephemeral preview environments across cloud providers' — a feature dimension cloud providers can replicate; positioning must shift to unique operational value or pricing collapses under commoditization pressure.","raised_by":["rethinkdb::devtools","parse::devtools"],"severity":"medium"},{"summary":"Tie engineering equity and milestones to revenue metrics, not adoption, to prevent acqui-hire exit.","applies_because":"The pitch is a developer-infrastructure product — exactly the profile that attracts acqui-hire interest from larger platforms; retaining the team through revenue-tied incentives prevents a strategic shutdown scenario.","raised_by":["rethinkdb::devtools","parse::devtools"],"severity":"medium"}],"injection_detected":false}
